per_deal_summaries.size: $3,500/mo up to 3K telemed visits + $2K implementation (down from $3,500) + $0.50/ClearPay + 1% Solv Pay; 6-month initial term (down from 1-year)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Abigail (ops/technical lead); Dr. Attia (clinical); Dr. Baba (decision-maker, skeptical)
per_deal_summaries.status: Contract targeting May 13–14 per Abby, who flagged 'don't want to lose momentum here.' Full end-to-end telehealth demo delivered May 5. Dr. Baba is engaged but guarding on ECW integration — explicitly said 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know.' Two sub-deals in play: UC Telemed (green light) and Specialties as a design partner target (Kapeesh call not yet scheduled). CLS feedback compiled into a doc for Maria Kwok (May 6). President of CLS is engaged and sees this potentially replacing Luma for all primary care scheduling.
key_risks: Dr. Baba has been burned by integrations before — ECW proof video and a Texas/multi-provider reference are required gate items
HMO/PCP billing logic unresolved — place-of-service 20 workaround unconfirmed; patients with assigned PCPs may not be billable
300+ providers at scale amplifies any ECW integration failure; CLS has seen Luma 'overload ECW with bots' and had to shut down parts of their integration
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley/Danny to deliver ECW integration demo video (HIPAA-blurred) and find ECW/multi-provider/Texas reference customer — due ASAP. Brantley to follow up with Abby on Dr. Baba referral partner call — this is the explicit contract gate. Kapeesh to schedule specialty scope call. Contract window is this week.

per_deal_summaries.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale; ~2K calls/week at 2 pilot locations (4 total; 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Jim (CEO); Deana Guy-Johnson (Clinical Manager, 10 yrs at GLOC)
per_deal_summaries.status: Signed April 27 — Solv's first Monarch design partner. Channel renamed #mon-partner-gloc May 1. Kickoff doc live. Kennedy handling Maya setup (DID phone numbers, routing), Carli handling Athena 3rd-party API access and preview instance. Kapeesh requested a Drive folder and design partner onboarding SOP to document learnings as they go. Jim's leadership team on vacation until ~May 16; formal kickoff call pending.
key_risks: Phase 1 is $0 — deal generates margin only once resolution rate hits ~35%, which is unproven in ortho
Deana (Clinical Manager) was openly skeptical AI can handle GLOC's complex scheduling rules — 20+ appointment types per surgeon
Org just 3x'd in size via two acquisitions; internal chaos could slow implementation cooperation
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to create Monarch Drive folder + design partner onboarding SOP (per Kapeesh, May 12). Kennedy to close Maya DID/phone routing requirements; Carli to close Athena 3rd-party auth + preview instance. Formal kickoff call post May 16. Owner: Brantley + implementation team.

per_deal_summaries.size: $800/mo per location + $1K one-time setup; 3 existing locs + new standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1
per_deal_summaries.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user); Christopher LaFave (Executive Director)
per_deal_summaries.status: Strong April 8–9 demo with full leadership team (4 attendees: Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). Team engaged and confirmed verbal interest — Christopher asked smart operational questions and said the workflows work for their urgent ortho model. Internal review done; team committed to reconnect in ~1 week. ECW integration proof is the hard gate — Christopher explicitly pushed to see data actually flowing into ECW. June 1 urgent care opening is a real forcing function.
key_risks: ECW integration confidence is a hard gate — Christopher needs to see live data flow, not just hear about it
HMO referral requirement unresolved for specialty (non-urgent) clinics — blocks expansion to 3 other ortho locations
ECW consistently flagged as a difficult integration; Jennifer noted they've been burned by tech partners before on ECW
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to send presentation deck + ECW integration documentation packet (was due Mon/Tues after Apr 12 call — may be overdue). Escalate HMO referral workflow gap to Kapeesh/product. Plymouth Bay team to review internally and reconnect; targeting within ~1 week of Apr 12 call.

per_deal_summaries.status: Formally paused since March 11. Jon proactively communicated (did not ghost) that Assort Health's contractual obligations block all movement until their vendor exit resolves — no timeline given. Deal was near-signature in late February before Assort surfaced. Jon confirmed intent to return and said they are 'very interested.' Last active check-in was April 7 — ~5 weeks overdue on the every-2-week passive cadence. No new update.
key_risks: Assort exit timeline is unknown and entirely outside Solv's control — could be weeks or months
5+ weeks since last touch — Jon's internal advocacy may erode over time
A competitor could step in during the open window
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to check in with Jon on Assort exit status — overdue, should happen this week. GLOC provides Athena ortho reference context that may be helpful when deal resumes. Owner: Brantley.

per_deal_summaries.size: 4 locations (3 new, 1 struggling Atlanta/Kennesaw location); MRR TBD
per_deal_summaries.champion: Randy Johansen (board member, former AFC CEO) — board-level, not operational
per_deal_summaries.status: Randy connected at UCA (April 20 happy hour intro'd by Heather). April 24 call: OS1 opened 3 locations simultaneously and is cash-constrained; the Atlanta location opened for months without an MRI and is actively hurting revenue. Randy agreed to share ClearPay + Maya materials with the OS1 team before an internal meeting. As of May 5 Granola notes, no response from Randy (1–2 day response window had passed). Low momentum.
key_risks: Cash constraints are real and could kill deal before it advances — opening 3 locs at once stressed the org
Randy is a board member, not an operator — no confirmed day-to-day champion driving it internally
Monthly internal meeting cadence means slow decision velocity even with interest
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to follow up with Randy; confirm if internal OS1 meeting happened and who the right operational contact is. Share ClearPay + Maya one-pager and ROI framing around cash flow. Owner: Brantley, with Evan on materials. Due: this week.

per_deal_summaries.size: 20 providers, 120 staff, 10-person contact center (expanding to 16), 6-month patient waitlist
per_deal_summaries.ehr: ModMed/gGastro (migrating from Athena; EMR go-live June 16, may slip)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Albert Lujan (Director, IT & Infrastructure) — controls vendor recommendations to the board
per_deal_summaries.status: Excellent May 11 discovery call. Albert is technically sharp, frustrated that every vendor has failed on ModMed/Gastro integration, and confirmed strong buyer intent — said if Solv can show a working POC, it's 'almost a done deal.' Core blocker: ModMed/gGastro lacks a write-back API per Jenn's prior research. Kapeesh flagged May 12 that gGastro is a distinct product and needs market sizing before Solv decides to build. Albert is also talking to a Chicago GI group that may have AI scheduling working on ModMed — this could surface a competitor. Brantley owes Albert a ModMed feasibility follow-up; the 1–2 day window may have passed.
key_risks: ModMed/gGastro write-back API gap is unproven — every prior vendor Albert talked to hit this wall
Chicago GI group competitor call could surface a working alternative before Solv confirms feasibility
June 16 EMR go-live is a tight window — missing it likely delays the deal by months
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to do gGastro market sizing and confirm integration feasibility with Kapeesh/Jenn — follow up with Albert within 1–2 days of decision. Window from May 11 call may have passed; follow-up is overdue. Owner: Brantley + Kapeesh.

per_deal_summaries.status: Cut off April 7. TPC won't replace their CTO-built scheduler ('too many resources poured into it'). They are open to Solv as an acquisition-only layer alongside their existing scheduler (GMB → Solv booking), but this is non-standard scope and Rikin flagged it isn't on their timeline for 6–9 months. Rikin is also piloting a competing voice agent. Brantley did not overcommit.
key_risks: Not on ICP for standard Monarch motion — requires custom acquisition-only deal structure
6–9 month timeline with competing voice agent already in pilot
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Closed out. Revisit Q4 2026 if acquisition-layer deal model gains leadership traction internally.

per_deal_summaries.champion: Osvaldo Ramirez (Administrator) — not the decision-maker; providers hold final authority
per_deal_summaries.status: Closed out April 7. No response since the February 18 intro call despite multiple follow-ups and consistent email opens. Providers are the actual decision-makers and were never directly engaged. Osvaldo was a mid-level champion only. Prior vendor burned them on hidden fees — trust was a prerequisite never fully established.
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Closed. No further action. Revisit only if Osvaldo re-engages post-Athena migration.

per_deal_summaries.status: Deal broken off by Solv in late March. No Dr. Chrono integration exists for ClearPay. Per-location pricing model didn't fit their 2-provider/6-location setup. Dr. Chrono as the EHR was flagged by Kapeesh as a blocker.

per_deal_summaries.deal_type: Expansion (existing Solv partner via Mederi Urgent Care)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator); Dr. Tovmassian (owner, also owns Mederi Urgent Care)
per_deal_summaries.status: Note: This is not a Monarch deal — ClearPay only, no Maya. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back March 16: 'Doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Point-of-care estimate workflow doesn't fit primary care with stable recurring patient mix. Brantley followed up requesting leadership call but received no response. Not pushing given this is an existing partner relationship.
key_risks: Real collection pain exists but product-market fit for ClearPay in primary care is unclear for this use case
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Not actively pursuing. Brantley to do one more gentle follow-up given partner relationship. No Monarch angle here.

leadership_table.key_notes: Contract targeting May 13–14; ECW proof video + Dr. Baba referral call are the explicit gate items.
Signed Apr 27; implementation underway; formal kickoff pending May 16 leadership return.
ECW live data demo is hard gate; June 1 urgent care opening is the forcing function.
Assort Health contract blocking since Mar 11; Jon check-in 5+ weeks overdue.
Randy (board) unresponsive since Apr 24; no operational champion; cash pressure is real.
Strong intent but ModMed write-back API unproven; competitor may surface; feasibility follow-up overdue.
Cut off Apr 7; won't replace home-built scheduler; 6–9 month horizon, competing voice pilot in progress.
Closed out Apr 7; decision-makers (providers) were never engaged; went dark.
Solv walked — no Dr. Chrono integration and per-location pricing model mismatch.
ClearPay only; Dr. Tovmassian rejected model for primary care — product-market fit miss.
